title: Yosys gives bogus error message on ternary operator with missing ? token
body:
Parsing this trivial Verilog module
```
module inverter(input a, output y);

   assign y = (a == 1'b0? 1'b1 : 1'b0);
   
endmodule // inverter
```
yosys gives the error
```
Parsing Verilog input from `/tmp/inverter.v' to AST representation.
/tmp/inverter.v:3: ERROR: Digit larger than 1 used in in base-2 constant.
```
If a space is inserted before the `?`, then the parse is successful.

Reproducable in current git master.
code_blocks:
Parsing Verilog input from `/tmp/inverter.v' to AST representation.
/tmp/inverter.v:3: ERROR: Digit larger than 1 used in in base-2 constant.
